finding: The Readiness and Status columns are just coloured dots (red/orange/blue/green) with no words, so I can't tell what each one means.

evidence.quote: Readiness and Status columns show coloured dots with no text label

persona_rationale: I read every word to feel safe; a dot with no label tells me nothing, so I can't tell whether a page is healthy or about to break.

suggested_fix.current: Coloured dot only in Readiness/Status cells
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a word beside each dot, e.g. a green dot with 'Published' and an orange dot with 'Needs work'

finding: Page web addresses are shown in tiny grey code that looks like a computer error rather than a page name.

evidence.quote: /resources/ux-seo-review-test-1781071765035

persona_rationale: Long code-looking addresses make me think something is broken; I'd rather see the plain page name.

suggested_fix.current: /resources/ux-seo-review-test-1781071765035 shown in monospace grey
suggested_fix.proposed: Lead with the friendly page title and show the address smaller and de-emphasised, or labelled 'Web address:'

finding: The page is titled 'SEO pages'; I don't know what 'SEO' means, so I wouldn't know this is where my website pages live.

evidence.quote: SEO pages

persona_rationale: An abbreviation I've never seen makes me unsure I'm even in the right place to edit my website.

suggested_fix.current: Heading 'SEO pages'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Website pages' with a small subtitle, e.g. 'Pages people find on search (SEO)'

finding: Step 1 of the create-page gate has a 'Continue' but no 'Cancel' or 'Back to pages', so if I opened it by mistake I don't see how to leave.

evidence.quote: Only a 'Continue' button at the bottom of step 1

persona_rationale: If I land somewhere by accident I need an obvious way out; without it I'm afraid pressing anything will start something I can't stop.

suggested_fix.current: Step 1 shows only 'Continue'
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Cancel' or 'Back to pages' link beside 'Continue' on every step

finding: Leaving the brand-new editor after typing a page title returns me to the list with no 'unsaved changes' warning, and my typing is silently lost.

evidence.quote: navigated straight to /admin/pages with NO unsaved-changes warning — typed title silently lost
Back on pages list after leaving unsaved editor

persona_rationale: Losing my work with no warning is the exact thing I'm terrified of; once it happens I'd never trust the tool again.

suggested_fix.current: Navigating away from an unsaved new page discards typing with no prompt
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a confirm dialog: 'You have unsaved changes. Leave without saving?' with 'Stay' and 'Leave' options

finding: 'Eyebrow' is used as a field label; I have no idea what that means on a web page.

evidence.quote: Eyebrow

persona_rationale: An unfamiliar word as a label stops me cold — I won't type into a box when I don't know what it's for.

suggested_fix.current: Field label 'Eyebrow'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Small label above the headline (optional)'

finding: The publish chip shows a terse riddle ('Improve SEO title' / 'Fix SEO title') instead of telling me plainly what to type and where.

evidence.quote: Improve SEO title
Fix SEO title

persona_rationale: 'Fix SEO title' is a command with no instructions; I don't know what an SEO title is, what's wrong, or where to go.

suggested_fix.current: 'Fix SEO title' chip
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Add a search title — write a short page name here, then it can go live' with a button that scrolls to and highlights the field

finding: Faint grey placeholder words on the page ('Hero headline', 'Hero body copy', 'CTA label') look like real text, so I can't tell what I'm supposed to replace versus leave alone.

evidence.quote: Ghosted grey 'Hero headline' and 'Hero body copy' on the canvas

persona_rationale: I can't tell instructions from real content; I'm afraid of typing over something important or leaving something blank by mistake.

suggested_fix.current: Grey placeholder text indistinguishable from content
suggested_fix.proposed: Mark empty required spots clearly, e.g. a dashed box labelled 'Type your headline here (required)'

finding: 'Slug' is the label for the web-address box; that word means nothing to me and sounds unpleasant.

evidence.quote: Slug

persona_rationale: A strange word as a label makes me distrust the box; I don't know what I'm allowed to change.

suggested_fix.current: Field label 'Slug'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Web address (the part after the slash)'

finding: The editor shows two busy columns plus a floating 'AI' button, 'Share' menu, 'Blocks', 'SEO', and 'Governance comments' all at once, so I don't know where to even start.

evidence.quote: Full editor with two crowded panels and many controls

persona_rationale: Too much on one screen overwhelms me; I freeze instead of acting because I can't find the one thing I'm meant to do.

suggested_fix.current: All editor panels and tools shown at once
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a simple guided 'Finish this page' checklist at the top that walks me through the few steps in order

finding: 'Governance comments' and 'Builder support' are headings I don't understand at all.

evidence.quote: Governance comments

persona_rationale: Corporate-sounding headings make me think this section isn't for me, so I avoid it even when I might need it.

suggested_fix.current: 'Governance comments', 'Builder support'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Private notes (only your team sees these)' and 'Help and resources'

finding: Some icon-only controls (the small eye and the move handle) have no words beside them, so I can't guess what they do without clicking and risking a change.

evidence.quote: Eye/visibility and move icons near the hero block with no text

persona_rationale: Mystery icons frighten me; I won't press something I can't read in case it breaks the page.

suggested_fix.current: Icon-only buttons (eye, move) with no visible label
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a short visible label or an always-on tooltip, e.g. 'Hide this' and 'Move this block'

finding: The revision preview has only a small 'Back to editor' link and nothing else, so I felt stranded and unsure whether I'd left the admin area.

evidence.quote: Bare page with single 'Back to editor' link top-right

persona_rationale: A bare page with one tiny link makes me feel lost; I need clear reassurance that I'm safe and how to get back.

suggested_fix.current: Single small 'Back to editor' link, no other framing
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a clear banner: 'This is a saved older version (read-only). Nothing here changes your page.' with a prominent 'Back to editor' button

finding: The only label is 'Revision preview / publish - Jun 10, 3:49 PM'; the lone word 'publish' doesn't tell me this is an older saved copy I can safely view.

evidence.quote: publish - Jun 10, 3:49 PM

persona_rationale: 'publish' on its own reads like a button or an action I might trigger; I can't tell I'm just looking.

suggested_fix.current: 'publish - Jun 10, 3:49 PM'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Saved when published on Jun 10 at 3:49 PM — you are only viewing this'

finding: The error 'Path must be root-relative.' is pure jargon and doesn't tell me what to type instead.

evidence.quote: Path must be root-relative.

persona_rationale: An error in words I don't understand leaves me stuck with no way forward; I'd abandon the task.

suggested_fix.current: 'Path must be root-relative.'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Start the address with a slash, like /resources/old-page'

finding: Once a redirect is created there is no delete or edit button in the table, so a typo is permanent from this screen.

evidence.quote: Redirects table has no delete/edit controls — created redirects cannot be removed from this UI.

persona_rationale: If I can't undo a mistake I'm too frightened to try at all; permanence with no undo is exactly what scares me.

suggested_fix.current: Redirects table has no row controls
suggested_fix.proposed: Add 'Edit' and 'Delete' (with an 'Are you sure?' confirm) to each redirect row

finding: The status choices ('Permanent move (301)', 'Temporary redirect (307)') include code numbers that confuse me and I don't know which to pick.

evidence.quote: Permanent move (301)

persona_rationale: The bracketed numbers look technical and make me doubt my choice even when the words almost make sense.

suggested_fix.current: 'Permanent move (301)' / 'Temporary redirect (307)'
suggested_fix.proposed: Default to 'Permanent move' with the recommended option marked, and hide the code numbers behind an 'Advanced' toggle

finding: The page is titled 'Redirects' which I don't understand on its own, though the subtitle helps a little.

evidence.quote: Send old page addresses to their new destinations.

persona_rationale: The word 'Redirects' alone is jargon; I rely on the plain subtitle to understand it, so the title should match that plainness.

suggested_fix.current: Heading 'Redirects'
suggested_fix.proposed: 'Send old links to new pages' with 'Redirects' as a smaller subtitle

finding: The heading and intro ('Compare every picker preview against the real resource-page render using mocked block content') are entirely developer language.

evidence.quote: Compare every picker preview against the real resource-page render using mocked block content.

persona_rationale: This reads like it's written for engineers; I can't tell if I've wandered somewhere I shouldn't be.

suggested_fix.current: Developer-facing audit copy shown to all admins
suggested_fix.proposed: Hide this page from ordinary editors, or label it clearly 'Developer tool — not needed for editing pages'

finding: This very long page of repeated 'Picker preview / Actual resource render' pairs has no 'Back' and no explanation of why an editor would need it.

evidence.quote: Repeated 'Picker preview' / 'Actual resource render' comparison blocks

persona_rationale: A long, purpose-less page with no way back makes me feel I've broken something or gotten lost.

suggested_fix.current: Long audit page with no exit
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Back to pages' link at the top and a one-line plain explanation of who this page is for

finding: The published page is just one headline, one orange button, and one FAQ — it looks empty and unfinished, but nothing warned me a page this thin shouldn't go live.

evidence.quote: Sparse live page: headline, single CTA button, one FAQ

persona_rationale: I'd be embarrassed to publish something that looks half-finished; the tool should warn me before it goes public.

suggested_fix.current: Thin page publishes with no warning
suggested_fix.proposed: Before publishing show a gentle note: 'This page is quite short. Add more content?' with 'Publish anyway' and 'Keep editing'

finding: The preview address is a long scramble that looks like a broken or suspicious link, not a safe preview to share.

evidence.quote: /resources/preview/2eSNnEXLh0znXOYIrUm2Hjk7ieSeIqY2P1z0wTAPiWw

persona_rationale: A jumbled web address looks dodgy to me; I'd be nervous to send it to anyone in case they think it's spam.

suggested_fix.current: Raw random token in the preview URL with no framing
suggested_fix.proposed: When sharing, show a short note 'This is a private preview link' so I trust it before sending

finding: A required 'CTA destination URL' field needed to publish is not on the page canvas at all — it is hidden inside a 'Block actions -> Edit settings' pop-up, so the publish blocker gave me no way to find it.

evidence.quote: CTA destination URL field only present inside the Block settings modal

persona_rationale: Being told to fix a field I cannot see anywhere on the page is a dead end; I'd never think to open a hidden settings pop-up.

suggested_fix.current: Publish blocker 'Fix content 1 · Destination URL' with the field only in a modal
suggested_fix.proposed: Make the blocker open the settings pop-up and highlight the exact field, e.g. a button 'Fix this — opens CTA link settings'

finding: After pressing 'Confirm publish' and seeing 'Changes published.', the yellow confirm box stays open still asking me to publish again, so I can't tell whether it worked or whether I must press again.

evidence.quote: 'Changes published.' toast shown while the 'Confirm publish' card remains open asking to replace the live version

persona_rationale: Two contradictory messages at once paralyses me; I might publish twice or assume it failed, and I'd be afraid I made a mess.

suggested_fix.current: Confirm-publish card stays open after a successful publish
suggested_fix.proposed: Close the confirm card on success and show a single clear state: 'Published. Your page is now live — View live page'

finding: After typing a date into 'Scheduled publish' and saving, the box went blank and no 'Scheduled for…' message appeared anywhere, so I couldn't tell if it worked.

evidence.quote: field re-rendered EMPTY and no 'Scheduled for …' status appeared anywhere in the editor
Scheduled publish box shows empty after saving

persona_rationale: With no confirmation I assume I failed and try again, or worse leave it not knowing if my page will publish itself when I'm not looking.

suggested_fix.current: Schedule field clears after save with no confirmation
suggested_fix.proposed: Keep the chosen date visible and show a clear banner 'Scheduled to publish on Jun 11 at 12:00 PM (your time)' with a 'Cancel schedule' button

finding: The scheduled-publish note says it 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)', which means nothing to me as an Australian and I can't work out what time my page would go live.

evidence.quote: Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles). Leave blank unless this page should publish later.

persona_rationale: A foreign timezone makes scheduling a guessing game; I'd be afraid my page goes live at the wrong time, like the middle of the night.

suggested_fix.current: 'Uses Pacific Time (America/Los_Angeles)'
suggested_fix.proposed: Use the viewer's own timezone (e.g. Australian time) and show it plainly: 'Will publish at this time in your timezone (ACST)'
